statement.p: A rectangles base is {7\ {\rm cm}} longer than its height. The rectangles area is {60\ {\rm cm^{2}}}. Find this rectangles dimensions.

solution.p: Let the rectangles height be x centimeters. Then its base is x+7 centimeters.
Its given that the rectangles area is {60\ {\rm cm^{2}}}, so we can write and solve the equation:
The polynomial can be factored, so we will solve this equation by factoring:
The solution x= -12 will not be considered, as a rectangles height must be positive. The height is {5\ {\rm cm}}.
Recall that the rectangles base is x+7 centimeters, where x is the height. Thus the base is {5\ {\rm cm}} + {7\ {\rm cm}}, or {12\ {\rm cm}}.
So the rectangles height is {5\ {\rm cm}}, and its base is {12\ {\rm cm}}.
